'''David R. Stephenson''' (3 December 1958 – 14 March 2022)<ref name="Forty20">{{cite magazine |title=Blackpool Power |last=Caplan |first=Phil |magazine=Forty20 |issue=4 |volume=12 |date=April 2022 |page=41}}</ref> was an English [[rugby union]] and professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1970s, 1980s, and 1990s. He played club level rugby union for [[Fylde Rugby Club]], and representative level rugby league for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], and at club level for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] (two spells), [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] and [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]], as a [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|wg}} or {{rlp|ce}}.

==Background==
Stephenson was born in [[Lytham St Annes]], [[Lancashire]], England, and he was a pupil at [[Arnold School]] in [[Blackpool]].  He was the son of professional footballer, [[Len Stephenson]].<ref name="Forty20"/>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Stephenson started his career as a [[rugby union]] player, and represented England at schoolboy level. He made his debut for [[Fylde RFC]] in 1977.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby codes unite in tribute to David Stephenson |url=https://www.blackpoolgazette.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/rugby-codes-unite-in-tribute-to-david-stephenson-3616632 |website=Blackpool Gazette |access-date=16 July 2023 |date=18 March 2022}}</ref> He switched to [[rugby league]] in 1979, joining [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].

===Wigan===
Stephenson left Salford for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in 1982 for a club record fee of £60,000. He won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at Wigan in 1982 against Australia (2 matches).

Stephenson played right-{{rlp|ce}} in the 15–4 victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[1982–83 League Cup (rugby league)|1982–83 John Player Trophy]] Final at [[Elland Road]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 22 January 1983.<ref name="Classic Match: 1983 John Player Trophy Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=33|title=Classic Match: 1983 John Player Trophy Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=7 April 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140407092528/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=33|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Stephenson played right-{{rlp|ce}}, in the 6–19 defeat by [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in the [[1984 Challenge Cup]] Final at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 5 May 1984.

Stephenson played right-{{rlp|ce}}, in the 18–26 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[1984 Lancashire Cup]] Final at [[Central Park (Wigan)|Central Park]], [[Wigan]], on Sunday 28 October 1984, played right-{{rlp|ce}}, and scored a [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]] in the 28–24 victory over [[Hull F.C.]] in [[1985 Challenge Cup]] Final at Wembley Stadium, London on Saturday 4 May 1985, in front of a crowd of 99,801.<ref name="1984-1985 Challenge Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=445|title=1984–1985 Challenge Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=16 October 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071016210443/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=445|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Stephenson played right-{{rlp|ce}}, and scored 3-goals in the 14–8 victory over [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at Central Park, Wigan on Sunday 6 October 1985,<ref name="1985 Tour Match: Wigan 14 New Zealand 8">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=722|title=1985 Tour Match: Wigan 14 New Zealand 8|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=24 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181024232310/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=722|url-status=dead}}</ref> played right-{{rlp|ce}}, and scored 7-goals in the 34–8 victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1985 Lancashire Cup]] Final at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]] on Sunday 13 October 1985,<ref name="1985-1986 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=685|title=1985–1986 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=26 September 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130926024611/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=685|url-status=dead}}</ref> played right-{{rlp|ce}}, and scored a goal in the 11–8 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[1985–86 League Cup (rugby league)|1985–86 John Player Special Trophy]] Final at Elland Road, Leeds on Saturday 11 January 1986.<ref name="1985-1986 John Player Special Trophy Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=675|title=1985–1986 John Player Special Trophy Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=12 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181012054114/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=675|url-status=dead}}</ref> He received Wigan's "Player of the Year" award for the [[1985–86 Rugby Football League season|1985–86]] season.

Stephenson played for Great Britain in 1986 against Australia, played right-{{rlp|ce}}, in the 27–6 victory over [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[1986 Lancashire Cup]] Final at Knowsley Road, St. Helens on Sunday 19 October 1986,<ref name="1986-1987 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=684|title=1986–1987 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=28 December 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131228114305/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=684|url-status=dead}}</ref> played {{rlp|rw}}, in the 18–4 victory over Warrington in the [[1986–87 League Cup (rugby league)|1986–87 John Player Special Trophy]] Final at [[Burnden Park]], [[Bolton]] on Saturday 10 January 1987,<ref name="1986-1987 John Player Special Trophy Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=447|title=1986–1987 John Player Special Trophy Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=28 December 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131228114252/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=447|url-status=dead}}</ref> played in the victory in the [[Rugby Football League Championship|Championship]] during the [[1986–87 Rugby Football League season|1986–87 season]], played right-{{rlp|ce}}, and scored a goal in the 8–0 victory over Warrington in the 1986–87 [[Rugby League Premiership|Premiership]] Final at [[Old Trafford]], Manchester on Sunday 17 May 1987.<ref name="1987 Premiership Trophy Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=686|title=1987 Premiership Trophy Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=26 September 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130926025455/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=686|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Stephenson played right-{{rlp|ce}}, and scored 4-goals for all of Wigan's points in the 8–4 victory over [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[1987 World Club Challenge]] at Central Park, Wigan on Wednesday 7 October 1987,<ref name="1987 World Club Challenge">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=452|title=1987 World Club Challenge|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=16 October 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071016210553/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=452|url-status=dead}}</ref> played right-{{rlp|ce}}, and scored a goal in the 28–16 victory over Warrington in the [[1987 Lancashire Cup]] Final at Knowsley Road, St Helens on Sunday 11 October 1987.<ref name="1987-1988 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=683|title=1987–1988 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012|archive-date=26 September 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130926024838/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=683|url-status=dead}}</ref> In 1987 Stephenson also represented Great Britain against France, and Papua New Guinea.

===Leeds===
In January 1988, Stephenson was signed by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] for a fee of £75,000.<ref>{{cite web |title=Leeds Rhinos mourn Yorkshire Cup winning hero David Stephenson |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-mourn-yorkshire-cup-winning-hero-david-stephenson-3613037 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post |access-date=16 July 2023 |date=15 March 2022}}</ref>

Stephenson played left-{{rlp|ce}} and scored 6-goals, setting the most goals in a [[RFL Yorkshire Cup|Yorkshire Cup]] Final record, in [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]' 33–12 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[1988 Yorkshire Cup]] Final at Elland Road, Leeds, on Sunday 16 October 1988. While at Leeds he played for Great Britain in 1988 against France, Papua New Guinea, Australia (2 matches), and New Zealand,<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and he was selected to go on the [[1988 Great Britain Lions tour]].
